message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>The rapid spread and high transmissibility of the Omicron variant of SARS-CoV-2 is likely to lead to a significant number of key workers testing positive simultaneously.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Under a policy of self-isolation after testing positive, this may lead to extreme staffing shortfalls at the same time as e.g. hospital admissions are peaking.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Using a model of individual infectiousness and testing with lateral flow tests (LFT), we evaluate test-to-release policies against conventional fixed-duration isolation policies in terms of excess days of infectiousness, days saved, and tests used.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>We find that the number of infectious days in the community can be reduced to almost zero by requiring at least 2 consecutive days of negative tests, regardless of the number of days’ wait until testing again after initially testing positive.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>On average, a policy of fewer days’ wait until initiating testing (e.g 3 or 5 days) results in more days saved vs. a 10-day isolation period, but also requires a greater number of tests.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Due to a lack of specific data on viral load progression, infectivity, and likelihood of testing positive by LFT over the course of an Omicron infection, we assume the same parameters as for pre-Omicron variants and explore the impact of a possible shorter proliferation phase.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:p>
title: Test to release from isolation after testing positive for SARS-CoV-2
